content: Every nation, at all times, makes decisions and takes actions based on its priorities. Issues deemed important will be addressed, regardless of how challenging they may be. Conversely, matters considered not important are ignored, even when they are easy to address. Ultimately, our politics determines which issues are prioritised and which are not.
comment: An often overlooked factor is execution capacity: even when an issue becomes a priority, institutions need the resources, incentives and accountability to turn decisions into outcomes.
